# UC-17.1.6 · MAC Authentication Bypass (MAB)

## Description

MAB devices bypass 802.1X and rely on MAC address only. Monitoring for unauthorized MACs prevents rogue device access.

## Value

MAB devices bypass 802.1X and rely on MAC address only. Monitoring for unauthorized MACs prevents rogue device access.

## Implementation

Maintain whitelist of approved MAB devices (printers, IP phones, IoT). Alert on unknown MAC addresses authenticating via MAB. Track MAB device population. Report on MAB vs 802.1X ratio for security posture.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, HPE Aruba ClearPass syslog, Forescout CounterACT syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: ISE MAB authentication logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Maintain whitelist of approved MAB devices (printers, IP phones, IoT). Alert on unknown MAC addresses authenticating via MAB. Track MAB device population. Report on MAB vs 802.1X ratio for security posture.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" auth_method="MAB"
| lookup approved_mab_devices.csv mac_address OUTPUT device_description, approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved!="Yes"
| table _time, endpoint_mac, switch, port, location
```

Understanding this SPL

**MAC Authentication Bypass (MAB)** — MAB devices bypass 802.1X and rely on MAC address only. Monitoring for unauthorized MACs prevents rogue device access.

Documented **Data sources**: ISE MAB authentication logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, HPE Aruba ClearPass syslog, Forescout CounterACT syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:auth. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(approved) OR approved!="Yes"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **MAC Authentication Bypass (MAB)**): table _time, endpoint_mac, switch, port, location

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unapproved MAB devices), Pie chart (MAB vs 802.1X), Bar chart (MAB by device type).

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth" auth_method="MAB"
| lookup approved_mab_devices.csv mac_address OUTPUT device_description, approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved!="Yes"
| table _time, endpoint_mac, switch, port, location
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (unapproved MAB devices), Pie chart (MAB vs 802.1X), Bar chart (MAB by device type).
